sliceTypes.name: Capability
sliceTypes.detail: User-visible workflow or domain behavior that should be planned and discussed as product scope.

sliceTypes.name: Coverage
sliceTypes.detail: Additional validation, permissions, edge-case, or alternate-state coverage for an existing capability.

sliceTypes.name: Evidence
sliceTypes.detail: Probe payloads, comparison artifacts, screenshots, drill-ins, or other observability work that supports a capability.

sliceTypes.name: Workbench Platform
sliceTypes.detail: Workbench runtime, orchestration, lifecycle, reporting, performance, and operator tooling.

sliceTypes.name: Seed Data
sliceTypes.detail: Gold dataset, target seed mapping, fixture anchors, and deterministic test data expansion.

sliceTypes.name: Hardening
sliceTypes.detail: Security, audit, authorization, reliability, cleanup, and production-readiness work.

rollups.name: Patient Chart And Portal
rollups.summary: Patient search, chart summary, demographics/contact/registration, insurance, immunizations, portal account/session/profile, secure messages, appointments, and generated report workflows.
rollups.operatorSignal: Treat the many portal and patient-chart slices as one patient-facing capability family with focused child coverage.

children.label: Chart, registration, and demographic mutation
children.detail: Core patient lookup/chart summary plus contact, demographics, registration, duplicate detection, deceased status, guardian, social, employer, provider, and care-team workflows.

children.label: Insurance, immunization, history, and care-team detail
children.detail: Focused patient chart subdomains that enrich the main chart capability without needing separate product-level milestones.

children.label: Patient portal account, profile, and secure-message workflows
children.detail: Portal account/reset/access/authentication/session/home/profile review plus secure-message inbox, compose, reply, thread, archive, read, pagination, notifications, and generated-report flows.

outstanding: Production identity hardening, MFA, and full portal key-management/decryption policy.
Broader portal attachments, routing queues, delivery integrations, and notification delivery audit.

rollups.name: Scheduling And Appointments
rollups.summary: Calendar visibility, appointment lifecycle mutations, recurrence handling, overlaps, reminders, and portal appointment request flows.
rollups.operatorSignal: This is the clearest example where many micro-slices should be read as one Scheduling capability.

children.label: Appointment visibility and lifecycle
children.detail: Read-only calendar plus create/cancel/delete, reschedule, arrival, checkout, no-show, category, pending, provider, facility, billing-location, and comments.

children.label: Recurrence and occurrence behavior
children.detail: Recurring metadata, generated occurrences, exception dates, occurrence cancel/restore/reschedule, root propagation, monthly/daily/workday/yearly/weekday/repeat-on rules, and series recurrence update.

children.label: Overlap, reminders, and portal appointment requests
children.detail: Provider/patient/room overlap tolerance, appointment reminder readiness, and portal appointment list/request/options flows.

outstanding: Stricter provider/resource availability validation and waitlist flows.
Billing/encounter conversion and reminder delivery/audit integration.

rollups.name: Encounters And Clinical Documentation
rollups.summary: Encounter clinical detail, mutation, metadata, sign-off, co-signature, amendment history, encounter-linked documents, fee-sheet, diagnosis, claim, and procedure linkage.
rollups.operatorSignal: Encounter document and billing/procedure link slices are child coverage for the encounter workspace, not separate product epics.

children.label: Encounter clinical detail and lifecycle
children.detail: Read-only SOAP/vitals detail plus create/update/delete encounter lifecycle, metadata mutation, sign-off, co-signature, and amendment-history readiness.

children.label: Encounter documents and attachments
children.detail: Encounter document attachment, upload, binary upload, scanned attachment, replacement revision, sign-off, denial, metadata, move, content replacement, archive/restore, lifecycle timeline, and external-link handling.

children.label: Encounter-linked revenue and procedure facts
children.detail: Encounter-linked billing, claim, procedure-order, diagnosis coding, fee-sheet entry, procedure-order entry, and procedure-result entry readiness.

outstanding: Templates, amendment policy controls, code search/validation, charge templates, and richer audit export.
Full historical document version chains and scanner hardware/OCR integration.

rollups.name: Documents And Attachments
rollups.summary: Patient and encounter documents, content retrieval, binary files, previews, thumbnails, scanned attachments, sign-off/denial, metadata, archive/restore, revisions, and external links.
rollups.operatorSignal: Document micro-slices should be grouped by document lifecycle and content behavior before discussing product completeness.

children.label: Patient document visibility and content access
children.detail: Read-only document lists, content retrieval, preview readiness, image/PDF preview, thumbnail, scanned attachment, and lifecycle timeline.

children.label: Patient document mutation lifecycle
children.detail: Create/archive/delete text and binary documents, sign-off, denial, metadata refiling, archive/restore, content replacement, replacement revision, and external-link filing.

children.label: Document authorization and protection
children.detail: Protected document access and add/write/delete authorization behavior for patient-document APIs and UI.

outstanding: Scanner-device ingestion, OCR extraction/queueing, external storage adapters, and fax/SMS integrations.
Full document versioning beyond focused replacement readiness.

rollups.name: Revenue Cycle
rollups.summary: Fee-sheet billing, diagnosis and modifier handling, claim status, payment posting, patient payments, account balance/aging/ledger/statement, statement generation/export, collections queue, and follow-up tasks.
rollups.operatorSignal: Revenue-cycle slices should be reviewed as one billing capability because individual charge, claim, payment, and statement slices are tightly coupled.

children.label: Fee-sheet and billing-line lifecycle
children.detail: Read-only fee-sheet plus billing line create/status/delete, diagnosis coding, charge correction, modifier handling, and billing authorization policy.

children.label: Claims, payments, and account ledger
children.detail: Claim status, claim mutation, payment posting, payment mutation, patient payment capture, account balance, aging, ledger, and statement readiness.

children.label: Statements and collections
children.detail: Patient statement generation, statement PDF export, statement batch candidates/packages, collections work queue, and follow-up task lifecycle.

outstanding: Claim generation, payer adjudication, remittance import, void history, and production clearinghouse workflows.
Deeper denial management, statement delivery, payment reversal policy, and audit history.

rollups.name: Labs And Procedures
rollups.summary: Procedure results, pending orders, procedure mutation, result/order/report correction, specimen detail, review queues, provider directory/configuration, order catalog, import, queue, and transmit readiness.
rollups.operatorSignal: Lab/procedure work is a parent capability with several child lanes: ordering, reporting, review queues, providers, and catalogs.

children.label: Procedure orders, reports, and results
children.detail: Completed results, pending scheduled orders, create/complete/report/result lifecycle, result correction, order correction, report correction, and specimen facts.

children.label: Review queues and sign-off workflow
children.detail: Report sign-off, bulk sign-off, reopen review, review queue rendering, patient/date/provider/lab filters, and queue transitions.

children.label: Lab providers, catalogs, import, queue, and transmit
children.detail: Provider catalog/directory/lifecycle/configuration/address-book, procedure order catalog, catalog lifecycle, vendor compendium import, order queue, and transmit readiness.

outstanding: HL7 generation, external lab acknowledgements, retry/failure queues, and production lab transport.
Specimen chain of custody, label printing, accessioning, reviewer assignment, and notification workflow.

rollups.name: Administration, Security, And Audit
rollups.summary: Administration directory, facility/user mutation, ACL read model, permission and group membership mutation, login/session/audit, protected directories, authorization policies, and role-sensitive API behavior.
rollups.operatorSignal: Security slices are intentionally granular for risk control, but they should roll up as one hardening capability.

children.label: Administration directory and user/facility lifecycle
children.detail: Administration directory, facility mutation, user mutation, and default active/inactive rendering behavior.

children.label: ACL read model and permission mutation
children.detail: Default ACL groups, visible permission objects, group-permission assignments, access-permission revoke/restore, and user group membership grant/revoke.

children.label: Login, session, protection, and authorization policies
children.detail: Admin login, failed-login audit rows, session lifecycle, protected audit/admin/report/patient/encounter/document/message/billing/procedure surfaces, and mutation authorization policies.

outstanding: Production ASP.NET Core Identity hardening, password management, MFA, and user-facility restriction matrices.
Comprehensive audit-log export and role-administration workflows.

rollups.name: Workbench Evidence And Operations
rollups.summary: Workbench app lifecycle management, test orchestration, progress ledger, timeline, comparison drill-ins, artifact links, reliability summaries, probe previews, and source/architecture views.
rollups.operatorSignal: Most high-numbered evidence slices should be understood as Workbench support for interpreting modernization quality, not new OpenEMR product scope.

children.label: Workbench runtime and managed applications
children.detail: Managed apps, Docker readiness guidance, start/stop/restart/start-all, route-scoped hydration, summary/detail app snapshots, and modern-ui-claude registration.

children.label: Timeline, progress, and capability reporting
children.detail: Paged changelog hydration, Project Timeline metrics, functionality progress ledger, weighted estimates, history chart, forecast, and capability rollups.

children.label: Comparison evidence and probe previews
children.detail: Comparison cards, drill-ins, safe artifact links, direct report links, screenshot thumbnails, normalized probe details, accepted differences, reliability summaries, safe text previews, and workflow JSON probe payload attachments.

outstanding: Long-term evidence retention policy, richer report exports, and deeper historical reliability analysis.
Additional workflow probe payload attachment backfill until all relevant parity suites carry previewable evidence.

rollups.name: Seed Data, Migration, And Production Readiness
rollups.summary: Gold synthetic dataset, canonical workflow anchors, deterministic reset behavior, seed mapping, migration planning, external integrations, deployment, privacy, and production operations.
rollups.operatorSignal: This rollup separates foundational test data and future production work from user-facing workflow capability slices.

children.label: Gold dataset and target seed mapping
children.detail: Deterministic 1,000-patient gold dataset with canonical IDs, workflow anchors, temporal coverage, and repeatable legacy/modernized seed projections.

children.label: Reset, cleanup, and run isolation
children.detail: Per-test reset plans, cleanup-backed workflow mutations, and evidence that temporary rows restore or remove target state after parity runs.

children.label: External integrations and production deployment
children.detail: Scanner/OCR, fax/SMS/email, clearinghouse, HL7/external lab, production identity, deployment, backup/restore, monitoring, migration rehearsals, and PHI/security controls.

outstanding: Formal migration rehearsals, production deployment model, backup/restore, observability, and incident procedures.
External integrations for lab, clearinghouse, scanner/OCR, fax/SMS/email, and portal notification delivery.
